question: Your aircraft weighs 5,560 lbs with a CG of 52.7". If your friend who weighs 175 lbs climbs into the backseat at a station of 72", what will the new CG be?

question: The VOR/DME approach to runway 15 at Martin State (KMTN) airport starts on a 14.7 DME arc with a descent from an altitude of 1,800' at R-039 to 22' at R-060 right over the runway end. question: A KC-135 Stratotanker weighing 268,000 lbs is transferring JP-8 (specific gravity of 6.5 lbs/gal) to a B-52 at a rate of 1,763 L/min. Each of the KC-135's four engines burns 3,200 lb/h. If the B-52 needs to take on 50,000 lbs of gas, how much time does the fuel transfer take?
Referring to the previous question: Including the fuel transfer and the fuel burn from the KC-135's engines, how much fuel drains from the KC-135's fuel tanks during the process?

question: Referring to the previous questions: The CG of the KC-135 before the fuel transfer was 23% MAC and the fuel transferred to the B-52 shifts the CG to 27% MAC. The pilot moves fuel from a tank located at 77% MAC to a tank at -7% MAC to move the CG back to 23% MAC. How much fuel was moved between the tanks (do not take into account the fuel burned by the KC-135 engines during this time)?

question: The price of fuel at the Niagara Falls Intl airport (KIAG) is $5.94 US/gallon and just across the border at the Niagara District airport in Canada (CYSN) the price of fuel is $1.94 CAN/liter. If the exchange rate is $1.34 Canadian to $1 US, where is it cheaper to fill your 40 gallon aircraft, and by how much?

You read in the POH for your Cessna 172 that in a glide, your descent gradient will be 600'/sm. If you are flying at 9,000' MSL and the terrain in the area is at 7,258 feet above sea level, what is your glide range?

The Lake County Airport in Leadville, CO (KLXV) is the highest airport in the lower 48 states at 9,933' elevation. If your aircraft limitations state that you may not takeoff if the density altitude is greater than 10,000' and the altimeter setting is 30.31" Hg, what is the maximum temperature it can be outside in order to legally depart from Leadville?
What is the ground speed of a plane that covers 47 nautical miles in 20 minutes?
An airplane is currently loaded to its max allowed gross weight of 2500 pounds, but its CG is aft of the allowed limit by 1 inch. Which of these solutions will bring the airplane within limits?

Upon reaching your cruise altitude of 1,500, you visually set your course for your destination, and notice that you have to fly a heading of 087°. What is your effective true airspeed if the Cub cruises at 75 mph TAS. Assume a standard atmosphere.

question: You are designing a fireworks display, and the launch area is 1.2 sm from the main viewing area. You need to synchronize the sound of the fireworks with the music that will be playing from speakers in the viewing area. What is the time delay between when the fireworks burst and when the sound reaches the viewing area? The night of the show is forecast to be 78°F.
You are climbing at a constant rate, at 15° nose up. If each of your two engines produces 8700 lbs. of thrust, what is the vertical component of thrust?

You are flying across Lake Michigan in your light twin, and you want to calculate your "point of no return" where it will be faster to continue to the other side than to return to your origin. The airports are 74 nm apart, and they are both right on the shore, so your entire flight will be over the water. Your true course is 079°, you will cruise at 4,500' at 148 TAS, and the winds aloft forecast says: [[TABLE]] Where is your point of no return?

Use the information from the previous question. While over the lake, you have an engine failure and slow down to your max single engine performance speed of 90 KIAS. You are 26.8 nm from the departure airport; both airports are at standard pressure. Is it faster to turn back or continue to the destination?
choice_a: It is faster to continue, you just barely crossed your point of no return.
choice_b: It is faster to turn back, your point of no return is 1 nm in front of you.
choice_c: It is faster to turn back, your point of no return is 4 nm in front of you.
choice_d: It is faster to turn back, your point of no return is 19 nm in front of you.
correct_choice: It is faster to turn back, your point of no return is 1 nm in front of you.

question: You depart on a quick scenic flight with full tanks and burn 850 lbs/hr. for 47 minutes. How many gallons of Jet-A will it take to top off the tanks when you land (assuming standard weight of Jet A)?

You are cruising at FL370 (ISA+4) at M0.70 and burning 1100 lbs/hr of Jet-A on each of your two engines. The FMS shows a headwind component of 34 kts. You are considering accelerating to M0.78, which would increase your fuel burn to 1400 lbs/hr per engine. How much extra fuel would you burn at M0.78 if there is 286 nm left in the flight? Disregard descent for these calculations.
The remote for your camera drone has a 200 meter range. What distance would the drone travel if you fly it in a complete circle at the maximum remote range?

After flying 21 minutes of your 113 nm flight, you calculate your ground speed as 123 kts, and notice that you are 3 miles south of course. If your original heading was 284°, what heading should you fly to go directly to the destination?

Flying through the Midwest, you are dodging building thunderstorms on the way to your destination. ATC approves a 20° left turn. Once past the weather, you turn 30° back to the right to proceed direct to your destination. At the time of your turn back on course, you've flown 128 miles. How far are you from your destination?

You finally upgraded to captain at your first airline job. Now that you have a little money, (after paying those student loans of course), you decide that it is time to have a little fun and get your tailwheel endorsement in a Piper Cub. This airplane is a lot slower than the regional jet you've been flying. While in the pattern, you look at your airspeed indicator which reads 75 miles per hour. How fast is that in knots?

The first landing was a little shaky, but now you're getting the hang of it. You come to a full stop right at your grass runway's intersection with the paved runway, which your instructor says is exactly 1/2 of a kilometer (He's from Australia). How many feet of runway did you use?

You've been signed off for your tailwheel endorsement, and now you want to fly the Cub solo. You're tired of the pattern, so you decide to fly out and back. Your chosen course is 340° with average winds of 270° at 24 knots. Your true airspeed will be an average of 70 miles per hour. You paid for an extra 2 hours of solo flight time. How long do you need to fly out to complete the flight in exactly 2.0 hours, including the taxi? Assume 0.2 hours of total taxi time.

What is your vertical speed if you are following a glideslope that results in descending 300 feet per nautical mile, with a ground speed of 128 knots?
On your most recent cross country, you covered 226 statute miles, while burning 85 liters of fuel at an average ground speed of 204 kilometers per hour. What was your average fuel burn?
The maximum allowable cross wind component for your aircraft is 35 knots, including gusts. Which of the following winds will allow you to take off from runway 34R?

You and your friend decided to do a little flying over summer vacation, so you went back to your local airport to rent the Piper Cub. Your friend rents a C-172, and you decided to race to your lunch stop at an airport 57 nautical miles away. The course to the airport is 165°, and the average winds are 290° at 22 knots. You know the Cub isn't quite as fast, so you get a head start over your friend. If your friend in the C-172 takes off 20 minutes after you in the Piper Cub, who wins the race? The average TAS for the Cub is 75 mph, and for the C-172 it is 110 knots.
choice_a: Your skills are so equal, it's a tie.
choice_b: The Cub barely wins, arriving 1 minute earlier.
choice_c: A strong victory for the legendary Cub, beating the Cessna by 3 minutes.
choice_d: Even that head start didn't help, the Cessna arrived 2 minutes earlier.
correct_choice: A strong victory for the legendary Cub, beating the Cessna by 3 minutes.
